channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Donald Trump will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell. It'll be a first for him
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to ring the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange, marking the ceremonial start of the day’s trading.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price And Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House voting on $895B defense bill with military pay raise, ban on transgender care for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — National defense would see a 1% increase in spending this fiscal year under a Pentagon policy bill that also gives a double-digit pay raise to about half of the enlisted service members in the military.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP Armed Services chair criticizes ban on gender-affirming care for minors in NDAA
item.description: House Armed Services Committee Chair Mike Rogers (R-Ala.) on Tuesday lamented the inclusion of a provision banning coverage of gender-affirming care for minors in the annual defense authorization bill, saying that Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) did not consult him about it.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Cornyn warns Hegseth faces 'unpleasant' confirmation hearing
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn (R-Texas) says Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s pick to head the Department of Defense, faces a nasty Senate confirmation hearing that would likely be uglier than the battle over Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis City Council adopts $1.88B budget for 2025
item.description: The $1.88 billion budget includes a 6.9 percent property tax levy increase — lower than the 8.1 percent increase proposed by the mayor.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: GOP senators say they won’t fight Trump on Jan. 6 pardons
item.description: Republican senators who witnessed firsthand the Jan. 6, 2021, ransacking of congressional offices now say it’s up to President-elect Donald Trump whether to pardon those protesters convicted of federal crimes.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden approves national security memo on China, Iran, North Korea and Russia ahead of Trump's return
item.description: President Joe Biden has approved a new national security memorandum that could serve as a road map for the incoming Trump administration as it looks to counter growing cooperation among China, Iran, North Korea and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Protesters attempt to stop removal of hundreds of migrants from public-funded housing
item.description: Activists and elected officials gathered outside Gov. Kathy Hochul’s office on Monday to protest the closure of two hotels housing hundreds of migrants in New York’s capital region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump names former El Salvador ambassador Ron Johnson as choice for Mexico envoy
item.description: Former CIA official Ronald Johnson was named by President-elect Trump on Tuesday as ambassador to Mexico during a social media post.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump is returning to the world stage. So is his trolling
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s recent dinner with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and his visit to Paris for the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral were not just exercises in policy and diplomacy. They were also prime trolling opportunities for Trump.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US says it will support new Syrian leaders who protect women and renounce terrorism
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday it will recognize and support a new Syrian government that renounces terrorism, destroys chemical weapons stocks and protects the rights of minorities and women. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said in a statement that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump-era Justice Department subpoenaed congressional staffers, watchdog finds
item.description: But the department's internal watchdog found no evidence of political motivation by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Mark Ruffalo presses Biden to act fast on 'forever chemicals' before Trump takes office
item.description: "The EPA has worked their butts off, against all odds, to get a drinking water standard on this particular chemical class," Ruffalo said.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s vow to end birthright citizenship could face legal challenges, GOP skeptics
item.description: President-elect Trump has doubled down on his vow to end birthright citizenship, a task that would likely face legal challenges as well as skepticism from within his own party. For starters, the 14th Amendment grants citizenship to those born in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk warns Republicans against standing in Trump's way — or his
item.description: Donald Trump’s second term comes with the specter of the world’s richest man serving as his political enforcer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Tulsi Gabbard on Capitol Hill to get Senate support
item.description: Two of President-elect Donald Trump's most controversial cabinet nominees are on Capitol Hill this week.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats frustrated with Biden's 'lame duck' end to presidency
item.description: With 42 days until President Joe Biden cedes power, some Democrats are exasperated at how he is spending his final days in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Republican eyes Medicare, Social Security for DOGE cuts
item.description: Rep. Mark Alford (R-Mo.) is floating Medicare and Social Security as potential areas of focus for President-elect Trump’s new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court rejects challenge to Boston's school admissions policy
item.description: The court's action was the second time the justices declined to intervene in an admissions program based on geography since their 2023 ruling invalidating affirmative action in higher education.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Minneapolis Mayor Frey poised to block council again as his veto use continues to climb
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed 15 actions since he first took office in 2018. That’s more vetoes than the previous two mayors combined.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez seeks to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) on Friday formally launched a bid to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress — a powerful seat that will play a prominent role in the party's effort to push back against President Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump’s position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC that focused on advertising likening Donald Trump’s stance on abortion to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: NYC plan would abolish income tax for certain residents: See if you qualify
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new proposal would abolish New York City’s personal income tax for nearly half a million qualifying residents.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
